title: Managed Database on Railway
description: Managed PostgreSQL on Railway with automated backups, persistent volumes, and native integration with Railway’s Git-based deployment workflow. Best for teams already hosting their application on Railway who want collocated database provisioning with minimal ops overhead.

cost.costNotes: Railway Starter plan provides $5 monthly credit covering small databases. Hobby plan at $5/month for predictable cost. Pro tier required for SLA guarantees.

provenance.estimatedMonthlyRangeUsd: Starter free credit covers very small databases. Most production usage $5-20/month on Hobby/Pro.
provenance.adoptionCost: 2-4 hours to configure Railway database, connection strings, and migration pipeline.
provenance.licenseCost: Railway charges for usage; no separate database license required.

provides.reasoning: Railway provides fully managed PostgreSQL with automated provisioning, connection pooling via PgBouncer, and Git-integrated deployment workflows.
Railway runs automated database backups at configurable intervals (5-60 minutes on paid tiers), supporting point-in-time recovery.
Railway-hosted PostgreSQL is standard Postgres; native RLS via CREATE POLICY is fully supported.
Railway managed PostgreSQL runs standard Postgres supporting PREPARE TRANSACTION for XA/2PC distributed transactions.

requires.reasoning: Railway is a SaaS platform requiring internet access for database connections.

availability.reason: Railway managed database available to any project using Railway as provider

supports_constraints.reason: Railway is a cloud-agnostic PaaS; applications deploy without cloud-specific infrastructure dependencies.
All major languages have PostgreSQL/MySQL client libraries compatible with Railway databases.
Managed databases serve all application platform types.
Pattern specifically requires Railway as the database provider.
Railway managed DB supports OLTP workloads; pattern compatible regardless of workload type flag.

supports_nfr.reason: Railway Hobby/Pro tier lacks multi-region failover and HA guarantees; max practical availability is 99%.
Railway's integrated database backups run at 5-60 minute intervals depending on tier, imposing a minimum 60-minute RPO on the free/hobby tier.
Railway database recovery and restart typically completes within 5-15 minutes.
Railway managed DB on hobby/pro tier supports up to ~500 read QPS before connection and resource limits apply.
Railway managed DB write throughput constrained by single-instance and connection pool limits to ~200 QPS.
Railway is not HIPAA-compliant infrastructure; HIPAA workloads require a provider with a BAA agreement.
GDPR-compliant deployments possible with appropriate Railway region selection and data handling configuration.
CCPA compliance achievable through application-level data rights implementation on Railway.
SOX compliance possible with appropriate audit logging and access controls configured at the application level.
Railway PostgreSQL supports read-your-writes consistency for single-instance deployments.
Strict durability achievable on paid Railway tiers with synchronous replication enabled.

warn_nfr.reason: Railway's hobby/pro tier database becomes cost-intensive above 300 read QPS; consider read replicas or a larger provider.
warn_nfr.message: db-managed-postgres--railway: peak read QPS is {actual} req/s (>300). Railway managed DB may become cost-intensive; consider read replicas or a dedicated database provider.

ssl_mode.description: Railway PostgreSQL enforces SSL on all connections.
